mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during periods of structural labor shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and rising consumer prices indicate stagflationary pressure that erodes corporate profit margins and consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL rises but RSAFS remains stable, or walk-forward Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate revenues, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns in >50% of expansion periods, or the signal reverses direction during supply-chain constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects broad economic expansion and rising corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth correlates negatively with 60-day forward returns in >40% of observations, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 3.5% forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and reduces real consumer purchasing power, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal loses statistical significance during supply-driven inflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate indicates a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and corporate revenue forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal predicts negative returns in >45% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power when automation reduces payroll sensitivity to economic growth.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts signal renewed construction activity and rising household wealth effects, which drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during post-recession recovery phases.
failure_condition: HOUST acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns in >50% of recovery periods, or the signal becomes negative during housing market correction regimes.
